coaching.coaching: Plan how AC grep patterns versus actual heading strings will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: AC grep patterns versus actual heading strings

coaching.coaching: Pin down ff-only failure modes and fallback paths with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: ff-only failure modes and fallback paths

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 3 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
verify command `grep -nF 'merge --ff-only origin/main' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'merge' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_merge'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"merge":')
verify command `grep -niF 'shared main checkout' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'shared' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_shared'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"shared":')

verify command `grep -nF 'merge --ff-only origin/main' CONTRIBUTING.md` greps the unanchored word 'merge' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_merge'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"merge":')

verify command `grep -niF 'shared main checkout' CONTRIBUTING.md` greps the unanchored word 'shared' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_shared'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"shared":')
verify command `grep -nF '2f.' CONTRIBUTING.md` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

AC item 'AC5 [operator-attested]: the change lands via Gerrit (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verif' cites operational evidence (merge_gate) that lives outside the codebase but is not tagged [operator-attested]; prefix the checkbox text with [operator-attested] so the completion verifier accepts a recorded attestation instead of failing to find code proof (ADR-0043).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC2's verify grep with the section label used in the plan body: either change the plan's label from `§2f` to `2f.` (e.g. `### 2f. After merge…`) or update the grep to `grep -nF '§2f' CONTRIBUTING.md` so both name the same entity.
evidence: Plan body names the section: '§2f ("After merge — fast-forward your worktree, THEN close the ticket")'
AC2 verify: `grep -nF '2f.' CONTRIBUTING.md`

If implementer renders heading as '### §2f After merge...' (without a trailing period after 2f), the grep returns no lines and AC2 is falsely reported as failing — or implementer writes '2f.' to satisfy the grep but diverges from the '§' prefix naming used in the plan
findings.finding: AC2's verify command (`grep -nF '2f.'`) uses a literal-dot suffix ('2f.') that may not match the section heading as described in the plan body ('§2f'), creating a gap where the heading exists but the grep silently fails.
scenarios: Implementer writes `## §2f After merge...` as a natural Markdown heading matching the plan description; `grep -nF '2f.'` finds nothing; AC2 appears to fail even though the work was done correctly.
Implementer writes `## 2f. After merge...` to satisfy the grep but the plan's own `§2f` label is never present, creating a terminology inconsistency in the rendered doc.
findings.suggested_fix: Choose one canonical label and use it everywhere: recommend `### 2f. After merge — fast-forward your worktree, THEN close the ticket` (drops the § prefix) so that `grep -nF '2f.'` reliably matches it; update the plan body's reference from '§2f' to '2f.' accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add non-happy-path guidance (in both AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md §2f) for what to do when `git merge --ff-only origin/main` fails — e.g., 'stop, surface the error to the operator, and do NOT attempt to reset or fast-forward any other checkout.'
evidence: Plan specifies only: 'git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main from inside the worktree'
No text addresses what to do when --ff-only exits non-zero
The original incident was caused by an agent improvising when the normal close path failed — same pattern can recur on a failed fast-forward
findings.finding: The new guidance covers only the happy path (fetch → ff-only merge → close). It does not specify what the contributor/agent should do if `git merge --ff-only origin/main` fails (e.g., worktree has local commits not yet merged, network unavailable, or the worktree HEAD has diverged), leaving agents without a defined fallback and at risk of improvising an approach that again touches the shared main checkout.
scenarios: Worktree has local commits on top of the pre-merge HEAD → --ff-only fails → agent has no instruction → improvises by resetting main checkout
Network is unavailable at close time → fetch fails → agent has no fallback path → ticket close attempt proceeds without the ff, hitting the same 'cannot confirm the work landed' error
findings.suggested_fix: After the `git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main` instruction, add: 'If `--ff-only` exits non-zero (diverged history, network failure, or dirty worktree), **stop** and surface the error to the operator — do NOT attempt `git reset`, `git checkout`, or any operation on the shared main checkout as a workaround.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In Plan §1, replace 'near the close-discipline bullet' with the explicit anchor: insert the new bullet immediately after the existing bullet that begins 'Work in a fresh worktree… run every subsequent command — edits, gates, rebar, the ticket close, and git — from inside it' (AGENTS.md line 130), so the post-merge ff guidance is co-located with the worktree-and-close instruction it extends.
evidence: Plan text: 'in the "Git workflow — land changes THROUGH GERRIT" section, near the close-discipline bullet, add a bullet'

AGENTS.md lines 130-133: the only close-adjacent bullet reads 'Work in a fresh worktree… run every subsequent command — edits, gates, rebar, the ticket close, and git — from inside it' — no bullet is self-labelled 'close-discipline'
ABSENCE: no other bullet in the Git workflow section explicitly governs the close step
findings.finding: The plan says to add the new AGENTS.md bullet 'near the close-discipline bullet' but the current AGENTS.md has no bullet explicitly labelled 'close-discipline'; the closest anchor is the existing line-130 bullet ('Work in a fresh worktree… run every subsequent command — edits, gates, rebar, the ticket close, and git — from inside it'). Without naming the anchor bullet, an implementer may insert the post-merge ff guidance in a poorly-visible location (e.g. after the 'Multi-story features' bullet at the end of the section), weakening discoverability for agents reading the section top-to-bottom.
findings.location: Plan §1 — AGENTS.md edit, 'near the close-discipline bullet'
scenarios: An implementer inserts the post-merge ff bullet after the 'Multi-story features' pointer (last bullet in the section), making it easy for an agent scanning top-to-bottom to miss before reaching the close step.
A future AGENTS.md edit re-orders the bullets; without a named anchor, the insertion point drifts.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'near the close-discipline bullet' with: 'immediately after the bullet beginning "Work in a fresh worktree… run every subsequent command — edits, gates, rebar, the ticket close, and git — from inside it" (currently the first bullet of the Git workflow section)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback decision rule to both the AGENTS.md bullet and CONTRIBUTING.md §2f for when `git merge --ff-only origin/main` fails (e.g., instruct the agent to inspect `git status`, and if the worktree has no local changes beyond the already-merged commit, use `git reset --hard origin/main` with a warning, or to open a fresh worktree from origin/main instead).
evidence: Plan §1: 'git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main from inside the worktree' — no failure path described
Plan §2: 'the same two-part guidance' in §2f — same omission propagated
T8(b): processing protocol (fast-forward-then-close) lacks a co-located decision rule for the failure branch of its key sub-step
T8(d): no fallback is specified when the fast-forward sub-step is incomplete/fails
git merge --ff-only exits non-zero when the branch cannot be fast-forwarded (local commits present, diverged history) — a realistic agent scenario given worktrees branched from stale bases
findings.finding: The agent-facing workflow protocol ('git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main') documented in both AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md §2f has no specified fallback when --ff-only fails (e.g., the worktree has local commits ahead of origin, or has diverged), leaving agents with an incomplete decision rule for this sub-step.
findings.location: Plan §1 (AGENTS.md bullet) and §2 (CONTRIBUTING.md §2f); AC1/AC2 grep targets
scenarios: Agent runs 'git merge --ff-only origin/main' in a worktree that has local commits not yet pushed; --ff-only fails; agent has no documented path (reset? rebase? abort close?).
Worktree was branched from an older origin/main tip; after the Gerrit rebase-if-necessary submit, the merge commit SHA differs; --ff-only fails with 'not possible to fast-forward'.

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
